commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Claude web recovery metadata, stores provider recovery actions with usage errors, renders a clickable `Re-login at claude.ai` menu item, and adds focused recovery-state tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main turns Claude web 401/403 into a static `Unauthorized. Your Claude session may have expired.` error and has no recovery-action plumbing. I did not run a live Claude/browser-cookie probe because repository policy restricts validation that could trigger Keychain or real-account prompts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor-provided real behavior proof is the remaining merge gate; automation cannot prove the contributor's visible macOS menu behavior for them.

target.title: fix(claude-web): surface actionable "Re-login at claude.ai" item when session expires
target.body:
## Problem

When CodexBar is in Claude **web** mode and claude.ai rejects the cached `sessionKey` with 401/403, the menu shows a static `Unauthorized. Your Claude session may have expired.` and offers no way out — the only real fix is for the user to re-login in their browser, but nothing in the UI says so. The menu also feels "frozen" while the main thread waits on the 401 each time it opens.

Fixes #1377.

## Solution

Make session-shaped Claude web failures carry an actionable recovery hint and let the menu render it as a clickable item.

1. **Backend (`ClaudeWebAPIFetcher`)** — add `recoveryURL` / `recoveryActionLabel` computed properties on `FetchError` for `.unauthorized`, `.noSessionKeyFound`, `.invalidSessionKey` → `https://claude.ai/` + `"Re-login at claude.ai"`.
2. **`ProviderRecoveryAction`** — new small value type in `CodexBarCore` with `init?(inspecting: Error)` that knows about `ClaudeWebAPIFetcher.FetchError`. A single extension point for future providers.
3. **`UsageStore`** — new `errorRecoveryActions: [UsageProvider: ProviderRecoveryAction]` plumbed through `menuObservationToken`, written / cleared in lock-step with `errors` inside `handleProviderFetchFailure`, with an `errorRecoveryAction(for:)` accessor for the UI.
4. **`StatusItemController+Menu`** — both the single-card and stacked-cards paths call `addRecoveryActionItemIfNeeded(to:provider:)`. The item targets `openProviderRecoveryURL(_:)` which routes through `NSWorkspace.shared.open` so it survives sandboxing. No-op when the provider has no error or the error has no recovery hint, so existing menus are unchanged.

The change is additive — existing `errors[provider]` semantics are preserved, no other provider's UI changes.

## Testing

`Tests/CodexBarTests/ClaudeWebUnauthorizedRecoveryTests.swift` covers:

- `.unauthorized` / `.noSessionKeyFound` / `.invalidSessionKey` → recovery URL `https://claude.ai/` + label `Re-login at claude.ai`.
- Non-session failures (`.invalidResponse`, `.noOrganization`, `.serverError`, `.notSupportedOnThisPlatform`) → no recovery hint.
- `ProviderRecoveryAction(inspecting:)` happy path on `FetchError.unauthorized` and negative path on `NSError` / `.invalidResponse`.

```
swift test --filter ClaudeWebUnauthorizedRecoveryTests   # 6/6 pass
swift test --filter ClaudeWeb                            # 13/13 pass (existing ClaudeWebRefreshResilienceTests unaffected)
swift build                                              # clean
```

## Screenshots

| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `Unauthorized. Your Claude session may have expired.` (dead string) | Same error + a separate `Re-login at claude.ai →` menu item that opens claude.ai |

(Screenshots to be added in a follow-up comment.)

## Out of scope / follow-ups

- Per-scope recovery hints in stacked cards (Codex segmented / Kilo scopes / Claude segmented) — currently uses provider-level hint, sufficient for the reported case.
- OAuth path (`claudeUsageDataSource = .api`) already has its own re-auth flow and is unaffected.
- The fragile string-compare fallback at `UsageStore+Refresh.swift:417` (`error.localizedDescription == FetchError.unauthorized.localizedDescription`) is left alone here; it would be a separate cleanup PR.
